description: Add correctness-focused stress tests to plugin-management, config-watcher, discovery-edge-cases, iife-injection, and reload-coalescing E2E files. Tests cover install/remove races, config convergence, broken plugin isolation, IIFE changes during dispatch, and coalescing precision.

userStories.title: Install + immediate remove race — final state MUST be uninstalled
userStories.description: Send plugin.install and plugin.remove concurrently. After both settle, the plugin MUST NOT be in the registry or config.
acceptanceCriteria: plugin.install and plugin.remove sent within 10ms of each other via Promise.allSettled
Both responses settle (either success or known error)
After both settle, health.pluginDetails does NOT contain the plugin
tools/list contains zero tools with the plugin's prefix
config.json localPlugins does NOT contain the plugin's path
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/plugin-management.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
A race where install's final rediscovery runs AFTER remove's rediscovery could re-add the plugin. The install/remove must serialize to a deterministic final state.

Changes:
Add stress test. Use a local minimal plugin for fast install (no npm). Send install and remove via the WebSocket JSON-RPC protocol concurrently. Verify final state.

Constraints:
- Use local plugin (not npm) for fast, deterministic install
- Either response may succeed or error — only the FINAL state matters
- Follow the existing plugin-management.e2e.ts WebSocket JSON-RPC pattern

userStories.title: 10 rapid config writes converge to last write within 5s
userStories.description: Write config.json 10 times with different plugin combinations. The final tools/list MUST exactly match the last-written config within 5s of the last write.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 config writes at ~200ms intervals with varying plugin combinations
The last write configures plugins alpha, beta, gamma (3 plugins)
Within 5s of the last write, tools/list contains exactly the tools from alpha, beta, gamma
No duplicate tools exist
No tools from intermediate configs that were removed in the final write
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/config-watcher.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The config watcher debounces file changes. This test proves the watcher reads file content at execution time (when debounce fires), not at detection time. If it reads at detection time, intermediate configs persist.

Changes:
Add stress test. Create 3 minimal plugins. Write 10 configs with different combinations. Verify convergence to the last write.

Constraints:
- Use createMinimalPlugin to create test plugins
- Do NOT send POST /reload — let the file watcher handle it naturally
- Wait for 'Config watcher: Watching' log before starting writes

userStories.title: 3 broken + 2 valid plugins — valid MUST load with exact tool counts, broken in failedPlugins
userStories.description: Configure 5 plugins: 3 broken (various failure modes) and 2 valid. All 3 broken MUST appear in failedPlugins. Both valid MUST load with their exact tool counts. Discovery MUST NOT short-circuit on first failure.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 broken plugins configured: missing tools.json, corrupt adapter IIFE, missing opentabs field in package.json
2 valid minimal plugins configured with known tool counts (2 and 3 tools)
failedPlugins.length >= 3
pluginDetails contains both valid plugins with exact toolCount values (2 and 3)
tools/list contains exactly the expected tools from valid plugins (total count verified)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/discovery-edge-cases.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The discovery pipeline must process all plugins even when some fail. If it aborts on the first error (uncaught exception in a loop), valid plugins after the broken one would be lost.

Changes:
Add stress test. Create 3 broken plugins manually (mkdir + partial files) and 2 valid plugins via createMinimalPlugin. Configure all 5 plus the standard e2e-test plugin. Start server, verify health.

Constraints:
- Broken plugins: (a) no dist/tools.json, (b) dist/adapter.iife.js with syntax error, (c) package.json missing opentabs field
- The tool count assertion must be EXACT (not >=) to prove no phantom tools

userStories.title: IIFE re-injection during active tool dispatch — tool completes, then new adapter works
userStories.description: Modify the adapter IIFE while a slow tool is executing. The in-flight call MUST complete (success or identifiable error, not hang). After settling, the new adapter MUST work.
acceptanceCriteria: slow_with_progress (5s) starts and is in-flight
Adapter IIFE file modified after 1s (adding a global marker)
File watcher detects change (log 'IIFE updated for' appears)
In-flight call resolves within 60s (no hang)
After settling, e2e-test_echo succeeds with the new adapter
The global marker from the new IIFE is present in the page
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/iife-injection.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
Re-injection tears down the old adapter while tool handlers may still be running. If the teardown kills the execution context, the dispatch promise may never resolve. This test proves the dispatch pipeline handles the race.

Changes:
Add stress test using setupIsolatedIifeTest. Start slow tool, modify IIFE file after 1s, wait for watcher log. Await the slow call. Then verify new adapter works.

Constraints:
- The IIFE modification must change the file content enough to change the hash (append a comment + set a global)
- The in-flight call may succeed (old adapter finished) or fail (re-injection interrupted it) — but it MUST NOT hang

userStories.title: 3 reloads within 500ms window produce exactly 1 discovery cycle
userStories.description: Fire 3 POST /reload at 0ms, 200ms, 400ms. Exactly 1 'Plugin discovery complete' log line MUST be emitted. All 3 requests MUST return 200. A 4th reload after the window MUST trigger a second cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 POST /reload requests fire at 0ms, 200ms, 400ms (within 500ms coalescing window)
All 3 return HTTP 200 with ok:true
'Plugin discovery complete' log count increases by exactly 1 (not 2 or 3)
A 4th reload sent 700ms after the 3rd triggers exactly 1 more discovery cycle
Final 'Plugin discovery complete' count is exactly baseline + 2
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/reload-coalescing.e2e.ts (add stress test)

Context:
The server coalesces reloads within a 500ms debounce window. This test proves the coalescing is exact: multiple requests within the window produce exactly 1 discovery, not 2 or 3. The log count is ground truth.

Changes:
Add stress test. Record baseline 'Plugin discovery complete' count. Fire 3 reloads at 0/200/400ms. Await all. Count log occurrences — must be baseline+1. Wait 700ms. Fire 4th reload. Count — must be baseline+2.

Constraints:
- Count 'Plugin discovery complete' by scanning server.logs array
- The 200ms spacing keeps all 3 within the 500ms window
- The 700ms gap after the 3rd ensures the 4th is in a new window
